files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptFont
files.Summary:
Very small PostScript font loader based on a custom text representation. The intention is to mimic a subset of the Type 1 syntax without parsing the full PostScript language. Each glyph is declared as shown below:

Glyph: A
Width: 500
Height: 600
Baseline: 0
Path:
M 0 0
L 10 0
...
EndGlyph

For a description of the real Type 1 format see Adobe Technical Note 5040.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.Type3Font

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.Type42Font

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable
files.Summary: The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CvtTable
files.Summary: The CVT (Control Value Table) contains a series of signed 16-bit integers that are used as control values during the hinting process. These values are used by the font program to adjust glyph outlines.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FpgmTable
files.Summary: The FPGM (Font Program) table contains a set of instructions (bytecode) that are executed once when the font is loaded. These instructions establish routines used for hinting glyphs.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GlyfTable
files.Summary: The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HheaTable
files.Summary: The 'hhea' table contains information needed to layout fonts whose characters are written horizontally, that is, either left-to-right or right-to-left. This table provides global metrics for the font, including typographic values such as ascent, descent, line gap, and other essential data used during glyph layout.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HmtxTable
files.Summary: The 'hmtx' table contains metric information for the horizontal layout of each glyph in the font. It begins with the hMetrics array, where each entry consists of the advance width and left side bearing for a glyph. The number of long horizontal metrics is specified in the 'hhea' table, and in a monospaced font, only one entry is required (but must still be present).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.KernTable
files.Summary: Represents the 'kern' table which contains kerning pairs for adjusting the spacing between glyphs in a TrueType font. This implementation supports the most common format (version 0) with a single horizontal kerning subtable.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LocaTable
files.Summary: The 'loca' table stores the offsets to the locations of the glyphs in the font relative to the beginning of the 'glyf' table. Its purpose is to provide quick access to the data for a particular glyph. For example, in the standard Macintosh glyph ordering, the character A is the 76th glyph in a font. The 'loca' table stores the offset from the start of the 'glyf' table to the position at which the data for each glyph can be found.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.MaxpTable
files.Summary: The 'maxp' table establishes the memory requirements for a font. It begins with a table version number and the number of glyphs in the font. The remaining entries set maximum values for various parameters (such as maximum points, contours, and storage), which are used to allocate resources for the font.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PostTable
files.Summary: The 'post' table contains information needed to use a TrueType font on a PostScript printer. It provides data for the FontInfo dictionary, the PostScript names for all glyphs, and memory usage information for the PostScript driver.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PrepTable
files.Summary: The PREP (Control Value Program) table contains instructions (bytecode) that are executed before rendering any glyph. These instructions typically perform scaling and other pre-processing needed for proper hinting.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.EquirectangularProjection<T>
files.Summary:
A simple Equirectangular (Plate Carrée) projection that uses degrees throughout:

X = longitude (in degrees) Y = latitude (in degrees)

Hence (0°, 0°) => (0,0) with no offsets or scalings. If latitude exceeds ±90, it is optionally clamped.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.GallsPetersProjection<T>
files.Summary:
Implements the Gall–Peters cylindrical equal-area projection "centered" at (lat=0°, lon=0°) => (x=0, y=0).

Formulas (in degrees): x = cos(45°) * longitude y = sin(latitude) / cos(45°)

lon = x / cos(45°) lat = asin( y * cos(45°) )

Both input (GeoPoint) and output (ProjectedPoint) use degrees.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.IProjectionTransformation<T>

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.LambertAzimuthalEqualArea<T>
files.Summary: Implements the Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection for spherical coordinates.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MercatorProjection<T>
files.Summary:
centered Mercator projection where (lat=0°, lon=0°) maps to (X=0, Y=0),

Forward transform: X = lon Y = ln( tan(45° + lat/2) )

Inverse transform: lon = X lat = 2 * [ atan( exp(Y) ) - 45° ]

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MillerProjection<T>

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MollweideProjection<T>
files.Summary:
Mollweide (Homalographic) equal-area projection, implemented with degree-based trigonometry.

Lat/Lon in degrees => (x,y) in "Mollweide units" with:

  • (0°,0°) maps to (0,0).
  • The bounding ellipse is roughly x ∈ [-2√2..+2√2], y ∈ [-√2..+√2].

Usage: var projector = new MollweideProjectionDegrees<double>(); var projected = projector.GeoPointToMapPoint(new GeoPoint<double>(latDeg, lonDeg)); var unprojected = projector.MapPointToGeoPoint(projected);

files.Title: Utils.IO.PartialStream
files.Summary:
Provides a view onto a restricted portion (a "slice") of an underlying . This partial stream starts at a specified offset within the base stream, and has a maximum length. Reading or writing beyond that partial length is not permitted.

The property reflects the offset within the partial stream, not the offset in the underlying stream. All read and write operations are bounded by the specified length. The underlying stream's position is restored after each operation, so external consumers of the base stream see no position change (though locking is used to ensure thread safety in concurrent scenarios).

By default, disposing this does not close or dispose the underlying stream.

files.Title: Utils.IO.StreamCopier
files.Summary:
A writable-only stream that copies written data to multiple target streams simultaneously.

This class implements both and so that the set of target streams can be dynamically modified at runtime. Note that no concurrency control is provided; if multiple threads write concurrently, external locking may be needed.

Reading and seeking are not supported. Calling will broadcast the provided data to all underlying streams in the targets collection.

By default, disposing this object does not dispose any of the contained streams. If the parameter is true when constructing this class, all target streams will be disposed when is called.

files.Title: Utils.IO.StreamValidator
files.Summary:
A write-only buffering stream that defers writing to a target stream until is called. Data written to this stream is held in an internal buffer. When is invoked, the buffered data is flushed into the underlying target stream. If is called, the buffered data is discarded without being written to the target.

This class does not support reading or seeking. After you finish all writes, you can either commit them by calling or discard them via . The underlying stream is not closed or disposed automatically when this stream is disposed (override if you need that behavior).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSText
files.Summary: Provides helper methods to convert DNS records to and from the textual representation commonly used in zone files.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSTextRecordAttribute
files.Summary: Specifies the textual format for a DNS record. The format string may contain property or field names enclosed in curly braces (e.g. {Preference}) that will be replaced when converting to or from text.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DigestTypes
files.Summary: Enumerates digest/hash algorithms for DNSSEC-related records, such as DS (Delegation Signer).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.FieldsSizeOptions
files.Summary: Enumerates the supported prefixed-size mechanisms for array or field lengths.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.GatewayType
files.Summary: Enumerates gateway types used in IPSECKEY records to indicate the nature of the gateway (e.g., an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or a domain name).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IANAddressFamily
files.Summary: Enumerates IANA address family types used in DNS-based IPSECKEY or other resource records that store numeric address family identifiers.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IDNSReader<T>
files.Summary: Describes a component capable of reading DNS headers from a transport-specific source.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IDNSWriter<T>
files.Summary: Represents a DNS serializer targeting a specific transport type.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IPSecAlgorithm
files.Summary: Enumerates cryptographic algorithms for IPSECKEY records.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.Protocol
files.Summary: Enumerates the protocols associated with a DNSKEY (or KEY) resource record, specifying how the key is intended to be used (e.g., for TLS, email, DNSSEC).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.Address
files.Summary: Represents an IP address record (A, AAAA, or NSAP) in DNS, as described by RFC 1035 §3.4.1 (A records), RFC 1886 (superseded but historically defining AAAA records), and the OSI NSAP address type. This class stores the IP address and reflects the correct numeric record ID based on the address family.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.CNAME
files.Summary: Represents a DNS CNAME (Canonical Name) record, used to alias one domain name to another, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.1.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.Default
files.Summary: Represents a fallback or "default" DNS record for scenarios where the record type is unrecognized, or when a custom processing path is desired for type code zero (which is reserved/unused). Despite the TXT-like comment, this class is annotated with [DNSRecord(DNSClass.IN, 0x00)], indicating it is treated as a placeholder for type 0. The raw payload is stored in the property.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.HINFO
files.Summary: Represents an HINFO (Host Information) record, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.2.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MB
files.Summary: Represents an MB (Mailbox) DNS record, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.3 (marked as experimental).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MD
files.Summary: Represents an MD (Mail Destination) record, which is an obsolete DNS record type as per RFC 1035 §3.3.4.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MF
files.Summary: Represents an MF (Mail Forwarder) record, an older mail-routing DNS record type now considered obsolete per RFC 1035 §3.3.5. Similar to MD, the MF record has been superseded by MX.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MG
files.Summary: Represents an MG (Mail Group) record, classified as experimental in RFC 1035 §3.3.6.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MINFO
files.Summary: Represents a MINFO record, considered experimental and largely obsolete as per RFC 1035 §3.3.7. The MINFO record provides mail-related information (responsible mailbox, error mailbox) for a mailing list or mailbox owner.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MR
files.Summary: Represents an MR (Mail Rename) record, marked as experimental and obsolete as per RFC 1035 §3.3.8.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MX
files.Summary: Represents an MX (Mail Exchange) record in the DNS, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.9 and further clarified by RFC 974. The MX record specifies a mail server responsible for accepting email on behalf of a domain, along with a preference value.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.NS
files.Summary: Represents an NS (Name Server) record as specified in RFC 1035 §3.3.11. An NS record indicates the authoritative name server for a given domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.NULL
files.Summary: Represents a NULL (experimental) DNS record as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.10. This record type can hold arbitrary data (up to 65535 bytes) and is primarily a placeholder for experimental or interim purposes.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.PTR
files.Summary: Represents a PTR (Pointer) record in DNS, as specified by RFC 1035 §3.3.12.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.SOA
files.Summary: Represents an SOA (Start of Authority) record, which defines zone-wide parameters including the primary name server, mailbox of the zone administrator, and various time intervals affecting caching and zone transfers, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.13.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.TXT
files.Summary: Represents a TXT record in DNS, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.14. TXT records store arbitrary human-readable text, often used for descriptive or application-specific data (e.g., SPF, DKIM, etc. in modern usage).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.WKS
files.Summary: Represents a WKS (Well Known Services) record, as specified by RFC 1035 §3.4.2. A WKS record details which well-known services are available for a given IPv4 address and IP protocol (e.g., TCP or UDP).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.AFSDB
files.Summary: Represents an AFSDB (Andrew File System Database) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §1.2. This record helps locate AFS or related database servers for a particular domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.ISDN
files.Summary: Represents an ISDN (Integrated Services Digital Network) DNS record, as originally defined in RFC 1183 §3.2. This record stores an ISDN telephone number (and optionally a subaddress).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.RP
files.Summary: Represents an RP (Responsible Person) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §2.2. This record identifies the mailbox of the person responsible for a particular DNS zone and an optional TXT record with additional information.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.RT
files.Summary: Represents an RT (Route Through) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §2.3. The RT record specifies an intermediate host that should be used when routing to the owner of the record, along with a preference value.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.X25
files.Summary: Represents an X.25 PSDN address record, as defined in RFC 1183 §3.1 (type code 19). This record stores a single X.25 PSDN address (e.g., a numeric string like "311061700956").

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1348.NSAP_PTR
files.Summary: Represents an NSAP-PTR record, which provides a mapping from a domain name to an NSAP address.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1712.GPOS
files.Summary: Represents a GPOS (Geographical Position) record as defined in RFC 1712. GPOS records store geographical location information for a domain, including longitude, latitude, and altitude.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1876.LOC
files.Summary: Represents a LOC (Location) record in DNS as defined in RFC 1876. The LOC record specifies the physical location associated with a domain name, including its geographical coordinates, altitude, and precision values.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2052.SRV
files.Summary: Represents an SRV (Service) record, which specifies the location of servers for a particular service and protocol within a domain. SRV records are defined in RFC 2782 (originally RFC 2052).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2230.KX
files.Summary: Represents a KX (Key Exchange) record in DNS as defined by RFC 2230. A KX record specifies a host that is willing to act as a mail exchange for the owner name, along with a preference value to indicate priority when multiple KX records are present.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.KEY
files.Summary: Represents a DNS KEY record (type = 25) as specified in RFC 2535 §3. The KEY record stores a public key and associated metadata (flags, protocol, algorithm).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.KeyOwner
files.Summary: Enumerates possible owner types stored in bits 6-7 of .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.NXT
files.Summary: Represents an NXT (Next) record in DNS as specified by RFC 2535 §5.1.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.SIG
files.Summary: Represents a SIG (Signature) record in DNS as defined in RFC 2535. The SIG record is the fundamental mechanism by which data is authenticated in the secure DNS. It digitally signs an RRset (as defined in RFC 2181) and binds the data to a specific time interval and the signer's domain name.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2671.OPT
files.Summary: Represents an OPT (Option) record as defined in RFC 2671. OPT records are used to convey additional options and information in DNS messages, most notably as part of the EDNS (Extension mechanisms for DNS) protocol.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2672.DNAME
files.Summary: Represents a DNAME (Delegation Name) record as defined in RFC 2672. A DNAME record provides redirection for an entire subtree of the DNS namespace.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2915.NAPTR
files.Summary: Represents a NAPTR (Naming Authority Pointer) record in DNS, as defined in RFC 2915. NAPTR records are used for flexible rewriting of domain names and are commonly employed in applications such as ENUM, VoIP, and Internet telephony to map telephone numbers to URIs.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC3123.APL
files.Summary: Represents an APL (Address Prefix List) record as defined in RFC 3123. An APL record is used to specify a list of address prefixes along with a negation flag, indicating which address ranges are included or excluded.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4025.IPSECKEY
files.Summary: Represents an IPSECKEY (IPsec Key) record, as defined in RFC 4025. This record can store IPsec-related data such as a precedence value, gateway type (IPv4, IPv6, domain name, or none), an IPsec algorithm, and a public key.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.DNSKEY
files.Summary: Represents a DNSKEY record as specified in RFC 4034. A DNSKEY record stores a public key used to verify DNSSEC signatures and is a critical component in the establishment of a chain of trust in DNSSEC.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.DS
files.Summary: Represents a DS (Delegation Signer) record as specified in RFC 4034. DS records are used to securely delegate authority from a parent zone to a child zone by providing a cryptographic digest of a DNSKEY record from the child zone.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.NSEC
files.Summary: Represents an NSEC (Next Secure) record as defined in RFC 4034. NSEC records are used in DNSSEC to provide authenticated denial of existence for a range of domain names and RR types. They create a chain of all the names in a zone, indicating which types exist for a given owner name and which names do not exist.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.RRSIG
files.Summary: Represents an RRSIG (Resource Record Signature) record as defined in RFC 4034. The RRSIG record is used in DNSSEC to authenticate RRsets by providing a digital signature that covers the RRset data.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4255.SSHFP
files.Summary: Represents an SSHFP (SSH Fingerprint) record as defined in RFC 4255. An SSHFP record stores a fingerprint of an SSH public key, allowing clients to verify the authenticity of an SSH server by comparing the fingerprint from DNS with the key provided during the SSH connection.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4398.CERT
files.Summary: Represents a CERT record, as specified in RFC 4398, "Storing Certificates in the DNS." CERT records are used to store certificates or Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) in DNS.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4701.DHCID
files.Summary: Represents a DHCID (DHCP Identifier) record as defined in RFC 4701. The DHCID record is used by DHCP servers and clients to associate a DHCP client's identity with a DNS name, enabling deterministic dynamic DNS updates for a zone.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5155.NSEC3
files.Summary: Represents an NSEC3 (Next Secure 3) record as defined in RFC 5155. NSEC3 records are used in DNSSEC to provide authenticated denial of existence while mitigating zone enumeration. They replace the earlier NSEC records by hashing the owner names.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5155.NSEC3PARAM
files.Summary: Represents the NSEC3PARAM resource record describing NSEC3 hashing parameters for a DNS zone, as defined in RFC 5155 §4.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5205.HIP
files.Summary: Represents a HIP (Host Identity Protocol) record as defined in RFC 5205. HIP records provide a way to associate a host identity with a public key and, optionally, a list of rendezvous servers. This mechanism is part of the Host Identity Protocol, which separates the roles of IP addresses as locators from host identities.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC6844.CAA
files.Summary: Represents a CAA (Certificate Authority Authorization) record, as defined in RFC 6844. A CAA record specifies which Certificate Authorities (CAs) are permitted to issue certificates for the owner’s domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC7553.URI
files.Summary: Represents a URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) record as defined in RFC 7553. A URI record allows a domain to publish one or more URIs, potentially with priority/weight for load balancing or backup links.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.SSHAlgorithm
files.Summary: Enumerates algorithm types for SSHFP records (SSH Fingerprint records in DNS).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.SSHFingerprintType
files.Summary: Enumerates fingerprint/hash algorithms for SSHFP records.

files.Title: Utils.Range.IntRange<T>
files.Summary:
Represents a collection of integer intervals (ranges), each of which may include negative or positive infinity as an endpoint.

Bitwise-like operators for set operations: | (Union), & (Intersection), ^ (Symmetric Difference), ~ (Complement).

Internally stores multiple non-overlapping intervals sorted by their (Min, Max). A null Minimum => -∞. A null Maximum => +∞.

files.Title: Utils.Range.Range<T>
files.Summary: Represents a range of values with a start and end, plus inclusivity flags. Stored as a struct for efficient copying.

files.Title: Utils.Range.RangeUtils
files.Summary: Provides extension methods for creating and manipulating ranges on read-only lists.

files.Title: Utils.Range.Ranges<T>
files.Summary:
Represents a set of intervals (ranges) for an ordered type T. Provides set-like operations (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference) via bitwise operators: | => Union, & => Intersection, ^ => Symmetric Difference, ~ => Complement (relative to entire domain, if desired),

  • => Difference (Except).

The intervals are stored internally as disjoint, sorted objects.

files.Title: Utils.Range.ReadOnlyRange<T>
files.Summary: Represents a read-only range of elements in a list, supporting custom stepping and indexing.

files.Title: Utils.Range.SingleRanges

files.Title: Utils.Reflection.ExtendedInvoker<TResult>

A cross-platform .resx reader that does not rely on System.Windows.Forms. It manually parses .resx XML to load both inline string data and external file references (similar to ResXFileRef).

This class implements so that it behaves like a read-only resource collection.
